bullish: Price is above the 20 EMA on the 4H
bearish: Price is below the 20 EMA on the 4H
neutral: Price is chopping around the 20 EMA

risk_rules: Only take trades where R:R is at least 1:2
No trading in the first 15 minutes of the NY session open
If I have 2 losing trades in a row, stop for the day
In Extreme Fear conditions (Fear & Greed < 20), reduce position size by 50% — bounces are likely squeeze-driven, not fundamental
Do not hold open limit orders during high-impact macro releases (NFP, CPI, Fed speeches, FOMC) — spread widens significantly in the 5-minute window around the event
For SOL specifically: require an extra confirmation candle before entering longs — it has been the weakest of the three structurally
April 15 tax deadline: expect elevated selling pressure and erratic volume — avoid chasing moves on April 15, treat any dip as noise until NY close
Around the April 22 ceasefire expiry: binary geopolitical risk — tighten stops or stay flat in the 24h window around that date
FOMC April 28-29: no new positions within 4h of the Fed decision — volatility will spike
notes: Market in geopolitical-driven recovery as of April 14, 2026. BTC back above $75K, ETH outperforming for first time in months, SOL stabilising. Three near-term risk events (April 15 tax / April 22 ceasefire / April 28-29 FOMC) could each cause 5–10% swings in either direction. Trade the setups, not the narrative — keep position sizes disciplined until BTC confirms above $75K on a daily close. Update this context block after each major event.

btc.etf_flows: BlackRock IBIT logged $269M single-day inflow on Apr 6 — 5-week high. Total BTC ETF inflow that day: $471M.
btc.sentiment: 56.7% shorts vs 43.3% longs — crowded short book, contrarian bullish signal
btc.note: Back above $75K today for the first time in over a month, driven by US-Iran peace talk optimism. Rally faces 3 tests: April 15 tax selling (~$2.8B estimated), April 22 ceasefire expiry, and FOMC April 28-29. Watch $75K as the line in the sand — hold = breakout potential toward $80K, fail = retest $70K.

sol.note: SOL stabilised above $81 support after weeks of weakness. DeFi activity is a genuine positive. Still needs to clear $88–90 to flip short-term structure bullish. Treat as highest-risk of the three until that level breaks.

macro_drivers_to_watch: US-Iran peace talks: today's rally is partly driven by renewed optimism — any breakdown sends crypto back toward $69K
April 15 US tax deadline: estimated $2.8B in crypto selling — expect chop or dip, not a trend day
April 22 ceasefire expiry: binary event — confirmation extends rally, breakdown = sharp risk-off
FOMC April 28-29: no rate cut expected — hawkish tone would pressure risk assets
CLARITY Act: 72% passage probability on Polymarket, Senate markup expected second half of April — positive catalyst for broader crypto regulation
SEC roundtable April 16: potential near-term regulatory clarity signal
BTC dominance at 57.3%: altcoin season historically starts when dominance falls below 50% — not there yet
Spot ETF flows: $471M single-day BTC inflow on Apr 6 signals institutional re-engagement — monitor weekly
key_dates_ahead: April 15 — US tax deadline (elevated selling pressure)
April 22 — US-Iran ceasefire expiry (binary geopolitical risk)
Late April — CLARITY Act Senate markup
market_context_2026_04_22.contrarian_signal: Fear & Greed below 10 for 46+ consecutive days — longest extreme fear streak in crypto history. Historical median 90-day return after similar streaks: +48.5%. Smart money is accumulating. This doesn't mean buy blindly, but it means the risk/reward on quality setups is skewed long on a multi-week horizon.
